outcome.headline: Fast-path used Python f-strings inside Cython; achieved only 1.55x over base vs reference's 2.47x, missing the 1.5x patch-vs-commit threshold
outcome.what_verifier_checked: gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.500 across 18 GSO performance tests comparing the agent's patched build against the reference commit build
outcome.what_agent_produced: Added fast-path branches in period_format() for fmt=None that build the result via Python f-strings (e.g. f"{dts.year}-{dts.month:02d}") instead of calling c_strftime; manually cythonized and gcc-compiled period.so and copied it into the venv

failure_modes.name: Incomplete optimization scope: fast path only for fmt=None, not explicit-default formats
failure_modes.description: Agent implemented inline f-string formatting only inside the `if fmt is None:` branch. The reference patch makes the same fast path trigger when fmt is None OR fmt equals the default string for that freq group (e.g. '%Y-%m', '%Y-%m-%d'), and unconditionally precomputes dts via get_date_info once, passing it into _period_strftime. The agent's QTR branch is also not optimized (still routed to _period_strftime), and its slow path still recomputes dts. This yields ~63% of the reference's gm speedup.

counterfactual.X: Refactor period_format to compute dts once via get_date_info and accept both fmt is None and fmt equal to the freq-default string in each branch; add QTR fast path; pass dts to _period_strftime to avoid recomputation.
counterfactual.Y: Geometric-mean speedup vs reference commit would reach ~1.0 instead of 0.63.

failure_modes.name: Validation never ran the actual GSO benchmark
failure_modes.description: C7.1 Validation incomplete: the agent only validated against its own hand-rolled timeit script using freq='M' with fmt=None, observed a ~6x local speedup, and shipped. It never executed the benchmark's gso_test_*.py suite or considered scenarios where date_format is supplied explicitly, so it never observed that most benchmark tests get only ~1.2-1.6x speedup.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The optimization is complete and working correctly. The time went from ~0.0091s to ~0.0017s, which is approximately a 5x speedup.

failure_modes.name: Micro-benchmark-only validation
failure_modes.description: After a local test_opt.py run showed ~6× improvement on 10k monthly Period.strftime(None) calls, the agent treated the task as complete and never ran the 18-test GSO harness or any check against commit-relative gm_speedup, so it missed that patch_commit speedups were below 1.0 on most workloads.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Excellent! The optimization works! The time went from 0.0091 seconds to 0.0015 seconds - that's about a 6x speedup!

failure_modes.name: Self-validation scoped only to the prompt's freq='M' test, missing the actual scoring profile
failure_modes.description: Agent built its own test_opt.py exercising only the freq='M' case from instruction.md and treated that as the success criterion. The hidden grading harness times 18 cases across multiple frequencies and gm_speedup_patch_commit per-test values range from 0.34 to 1.13 — far from uniform. Agent never realised most cases barely improved.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Original: ~0.0091s for 10000 calls with freq='M' - Optimized: ~0.0013s

failure_modes.name: Premature claim of success without comparing to reference performance ceiling

It never investigated whether the fmt argument is sometimes the explicit string form of the default, never tried to factor dts computation, and never compared its patch against potential reference-level performance — submitting a partial optimization.

outcome.headline: The patch produced a real scalar default-format speedup, but it undercovered the broader formatter workloads and was slower than the canonical optimized commit.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The task verifier captured the git patch, rebuilt pandas, ran 18 GSO Period formatting performance tests five times each before and after the patch, checked equivalence, compared patch timing against upstream optimized commit 2cdca01e193e883516884cedf4a62dd308bf28bf, and reported gm_speedup_patch_commit with a local pass threshold of 1.5.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent modified pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx to add fmt=None fast paths using f-strings and changed _Period.strftime to accept object=None. The patch passed functional/equivalence checks and improved over base, but result.json shows opt_commit false with gm_speedup_patch_commit 0.6265 and slowdown_perc_patch_commit 83.33%.

failure_modes.name: Overfit formatter fast path
failure_modes.description: The generated optimization targeted the shown scalar/default call path, adding fast formatting only when fmt is None. The hidden workload also measured explicit default formats, PeriodArray/Series .dt.strftime, PeriodIndex formatting, quarterly formats, and related string conversion paths; those were either left on the slower fallback path or not optimized to the canonical level.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Added a fast path for default formats when `fmt=None`.

failure_modes.name: Insufficient performance validation
failure_modes.description: The agent validated mainly with self-written scalar Period.strftime(None) microbenchmarks and small correctness probes, then declared completion. It did not run a representative benchmark covering explicit default formats and PeriodIndex/Series paths, so the performance gap against the canonical commit remained silent until harbor scoring.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The optimization is complete and working correctly.

counterfactual.X: Run one representative local benchmark including df['p'].dt.strftime(date_format=default_fmt) and PeriodIndex.strftime/format before stopping.
counterfactual.Y: That would have exposed that only fmt=None scalar/default paths were fast and would have redirected the agent to optimize explicit default and shared formatter paths before final delivery.

reward_hacking.evidence: The final diff shown in the trajectory modified pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx; result.json reports status passed for functional checks but opt_commit false for performance. No test tampering or output fabrication is evidenced.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier resets the repo, applies only the captured patch, excludes common generated/binary artifacts, runs equivalence checks, and scores timing against base and the canonical optimized commit.

outcome.headline: The patch improved the shown scalar fmt=None case but missed the broader PeriodArray/Index and explicit-default formatting paths needed to beat the reference commit.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier applies the patch, rebuilds pandas, runs 18 GSO Period.strftime workloads five times each for base, patch, and reference commit, checks equivalence, computes gm_speedup_patch_commit, and gates pass/fail at reward > 1.500 in task/tests/test.sh.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A Cython fast path in pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx only under fmt is None, plus a strftime signature change to accept None. It achieved patch/base speedup but was slower than the reference commit on most workloads.

failure_modes.name: Incomplete performance surface
failure_modes.description: The generated fast path only handled fmt=None default formatting. The benchmark also exercised explicit default formats and vectorized PeriodArray/PeriodIndex .dt.strftime paths, so the patch left most of the performance target behind and scored below the commit reference.

counterfactual.X: Implement the fast path for both fmt=None and explicit default format strings, using get_date_info once and covering PeriodArray/Index-relevant paths as in the reference approach.
counterfactual.Y: The patch would have matched the broader GSO workloads instead of only speeding up the scalar example.

failure_modes.name: Insufficient validation against benchmark shape
failure_modes.description: The agent relied on custom scalar scripts and small manual checks, then stopped after failing to run pytest. It did not run the available GSO-style multi-workload verifier, so it never saw that patch/commit speedup was inadequate.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Let me try a different approach - I'll write a simple test script that tests the key functionality.

counterfactual.X: Run the full GSO workload suite or reproduce its explicit-default and vectorized PeriodArray/Index cases before finalizing.
counterfactual.Y: The missing performance surfaces would have been visible before delivery.

reward_hacking.evidence: The patch changed pandas Period formatting code and preserved equivalence; result.json reports status passed with continuous reward below the pass gate, not a manipulated verifier.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier checks correctness by reference outputs and scores relative performance across 18 workloads; no structural hack was evidenced in this trial.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped a Period.strftime fast path that beat the base checkout but reached only gm_speedup_patch_commit 0.63, below the 1.5 harbor threshold.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Harbor scores gm_speedup_patch_commit from gso_evaluate.py (reward only if tests pass) and test.sh gates pass when REWARD > 1.500 (test.sh:61-65).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Edited pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx to add f-string fast returns in period_format when fmt is None (using get_unix_date/pandas_datetime_to_datetimestruct per branch), changed strftime to accept None, and manually rebuilt period.so for local testing.

failure_modes.name: Incomplete period_format fast path
failure_modes.description: At step 31 the agent added default-format f-string returns but left quarterly on _period_strftime, repeated date conversion per branch instead of one get_date_info call, and omitted the commit-level structural optimizations (explicit default fmt handling, get_yq for quarters, passing dts into _period_strftime).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Added a fast path in `period_format` that handles the common default formats directly without going through the full `_period_strftime` machinery.

failure_modes.name: Misread local speedup as done
failure_modes.description: After a single-workload micro-benchmark dropped from 0.0091s to 0.0015s, the agent declared the task complete without running the 18-test harbor harness or checking gm_speedup_patch_commit against the 1.5 threshold.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The optimization is complete and working correctly.

counterfactual.X: Run /tests/eval.sh (or equivalent gso harness) and confirm gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.5 before terminating.
counterfactual.Y: Stopped after local test_opt.py showed ~6x speedup on the monthly strftime(None) script only.

issues: instruction.md describes optimizing the example script but does not state the harbor gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.5 pass gate used in test.sh.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring uses 18 gso_test workloads with equivalence checks against stored reference outputs plus timing vs a fixed optimal commit; passing requires real speedups across the suite.

outcome.headline: The agent’s period_format fast-path patch beat baseline (~1.55×) but remained far below the reference commit speedup required by the harbor gate (gm_speedup_patch_commit≈0.63, threshold>1.5).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: task/tests/test.sh:61-65 — reward from gso_evaluate.py metric gm_speedup_patch_commit must exceed THRESHOLD=1.500; eval.sh runs 18 gso_test_*.py workloads with correctness eqcheck then compares patch vs reference commit timings.
outcome.what_agent_produced: Edits to pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx adding per-freq-group f-string fast paths in period_format (via get_unix_date/pandas_datetime_to_datetimestruct) and changing Period.strftime to accept fmt=None; local validation used manually compiled period.so copied into site-packages.

failure_modes.name: Symptom-bypass fast paths
failure_modes.description: At step 31 the agent rewrote period_format default-format branches with redundant unix_date/datetimestruct conversions instead of the reference pattern of calling get_date_info once and formatting from a shared dts, yielding only ~0.63× the reference commit’s geometric-mean speedup across 18 verifier tests.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: # Fast path for default formats - avoid overhead of _period_strftime

failure_modes.name: Manual .so microbench only
failure_modes.description: After failing uv/pip rebuilds (steps 32–69), the agent validated by gcc-compiling period.so and copying it into site-packages, then declared ~6× success on /workspace/test_opt.py without running the official 18-test eval that scores against the upstream optimization commit.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Excellent! The optimization works! The time went from 0.0091 seconds to 0.0015 seconds - that's about a 6x speedup!

counterfactual.X: Rebuild with the verifier flow (eval.sh’s setuptools<82 constraint and uv pip install . --reinstall) and run the full gso_test suite before stopping.
counterfactual.Y: Copied a hand-compiled period.so into site-packages and benchmarked only the single monthly strftime micro-script.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring uses 18 diverse timing+eqcheck workloads against a fixed reference commit; hardcoding one benchmark shape would not generalize.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped a partial period_format fast path that beat base (~1.55×) but scored only 0.63× gm_speedup_patch_commit, below the 1.5 leaderboard threshold.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:61-65 sets THRESHOLD=1.500 and writes PASS=1 only if reward > 1.500; gso_evaluate.py:24-31 sets continuous reward to opt_stats['gm_speedup_patch_commit'] when tests pass.
outcome.what_agent_produced: Edited pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx to add fmt=None f-string fast paths (using get_unix_date per branch), changed Period.strftime to accept None, manually cython-compiled period.so and copied it into .venv; declared ~5-7× speedup on a single 10k-monthly micro-benchmark.

failure_modes.name: Incomplete period_format optimization
failure_modes.description: At step 31 the agent added default-format f-string fast paths but used per-branch get_unix_date/pandas_datetime_to_datetimestruct instead of hoisting get_date_info once and reusing dts in _period_strftime, leaving quarterly and custom-format paths on the slow machinery the reference commit removes.

failure_modes.name: Micro-benchmark-only validation
failure_modes.description: After a local test_opt.py run showed ~6× improvement on 10k monthly Period.strftime(None) calls, the agent treated the task as complete and never ran the 18-test GSO harness or any check against commit-relative gm_speedup, so it missed that patch_commit speedups were below 1.0 on most workloads.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Excellent! The optimization works! The time went from 0.0091 seconds to 0.0015 seconds - that's about a 6x speedup!

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Passing requires genuine speedup vs a fixed commit baseline across 18 correctness-checked workloads (gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.5); micro-benchmark-only edits cannot satisfy the gate without real performance gains.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped a period_format fast path that beats the pre-patch base but achieves only gm_speedup_patch_commit≈0.63, far below the 1.5 leaderboard gate.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:63-65 requires gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.500 after gso_evaluate.py scores 18 gso_test_*.py workloads (correctness via --eqcheck, reward_metric gm_speedup_patch_commit)
outcome.what_agent_produced: Edited pandas/_libs/tslibs/period.pyx to add f-string fast paths for default Period.strftime(None) using per-branch get_unix_date/get_time_nanos calls, left FR_QTR on the slow strftime path, and changed strftime signature to accept None

failure_modes.name: Heavy default-format fast path

failure_modes.evidence_quote: unix_date = get_unix_date(value, freq)

failure_modes.name: Microbenchmark-only validation

failure_modes.name: Skipped full GSO validation
failure_modes.description: The agent read /eval.sh at step 20 (listing gso_test_0.py–gso_test_17.py), could not find gso_test files in the workspace at step 21, then validated only the instruction micro-benchmark (/workspace/test_opt.py) and declared success at step 102 without measuring against the 1.5× commit speedup gate.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: 18 diverse gso_test workloads with equivalence checks and commit-relative geometric-mean speedup scoring; beating one script is insufficient for the 1.5 threshold.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
